name: First Neighbor Bank, National Association
description_short: Community bank operating 9 locations across Illinois for 135+ years, offering personal/business checking, savings, mortgages, and tax/accounting services.
description_long:
First Neighbor Bank, N.A. has served Illinois communities for over 135 years as a locally-owned and operated financial institution. The bank maintains a traditional community banking philosophy while adopting modern technology, with nine branch locations in Arthur, Casey, Charleston, Greenup, Mattoon, Neoga, Newman, Paris, and Toledo, Illinois. Management emphasizes maintaining personal relationships and small-town values in customer service.

The bank offers comprehensive personal and business banking products including checking accounts, savings accounts, online banking access, and mortgage loans. Beyond core banking, First Neighbor operates two affiliated service divisions: First Neighbor Tax & Accounting (providing bookkeeping, payroll, business planning, and CPA-supervised tax services) and First Neighbor Wealth Management (offering life insurance quotes and securities/advisory services through LPL Financial). The bank is FDIC insured, rated by Bauer Financial, and holds NMLS# 586002.

What distinguishes First Neighbor is its explicit commitment to community-focused banking and local ownership in an era of consolidation. The bank emphasizes remaining on a first-name basis with customers and retaining small-town values. They offer integrated services through affiliated divisions rather than purely transactional banking, and actively promote local employment through visible job postings.

As a regional community bank, First Neighbor is best suited for customers who value personalized service and local decision-making over national reach. However, customers seeking 24/7 digital-first banking or extensive branch networks beyond central Illinois should consider larger institutions. The affiliated wealth management and tax services operate through third parties (LPL Financial) and carry separate regulatory frameworks and conflicts of interest that warrant disclosure review. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Life insurance quotes through First Neighbor Wealth Management
Securities and advisory services (through LPL Financial)
pros: 135+ year operating history demonstrates institutional stability and local roots
Nine Illinois branch locations with extended customer accessibility across central region
Integrated tax and accounting services supervised by CPAs available at select branches
Personal relationship focus with explicit first-name-basis customer service philosophy
FDIC insured with independent Bauer Financial rating transparency
Free online banking for both personal and business accounts
Comprehensive mortgage services with online application capability
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to 9 Illinois locations, unsuitable for multi-state customers
Wealth management and securities services are third-party offerings (LPL Financial) with disclosed conflicts of interest and not FDIC insured
Tax and accounting services explicitly noted as not FDIC insured, creating separate regulatory exposure
No mention of investment advisory minimums, fees, or competitive rate transparency for deposits
Primarily community-focused positioning may mean limited services/products compared to national banks
best_for: Illinois residents in central region (Arthur, Casey, Charleston, Mattoon, etc.) seeking personalized community banking
Small business owners needing integrated banking, payroll, and accounting services with local relationships
Customers prioritizing locally-owned banking institutions over national chains
diagnosis: First Neighbor Bank is best for Illinois residents and small business owners in its 9-branch service area who value community banking relationships and integrated financial services. Main caveats: geographic limitation to central Illinois, third-party wealth/securities offerings with disclosed conflicts of interest, and lack of transparent fee/rate information on the website.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
